title: Would you have reestablished the AT fields
display_title: Would you have reestablished the AT fields
wikitext:
[]: Would you have reestablished the AT-fields? Post by consrv4us » Thu Sep 07, 2006 6:32 pm Sometimes I wonder if Shinji's desicion to discontinue the human instrumentality project was the best choice. I mean there is a real upside to the evolution that the SEELE people envisioned. One, there would be no more suffering. Two, everybody would be equal once and for all. Three, lastly without the dependency on the protien based bodies of our physical self the survival of humanity itself would almost be ensured.

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Sep 07, 2006 6:40 pm Yes, but you lose the freedom of being an individual. In short, would you give up your freedom to think for yourself to live forever?

[]: Post by BobBQ » Thu Sep 07, 2006 6:52 pm Becoming a Borg drone offers the same benefits, but would you consider that a good thing? I wouldn't; their network security sucks and the lag times are awful. No creativity, either.

[]: Post by Rawinder » Thu Sep 07, 2006 7:34 pm I probably wouldn't. Eternal life in a world of my own creation sounds better.

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Sep 07, 2006 7:45 pm Nah. I would. I'd rather die than lose the ability to think for myself.

[]: Post by ChronoX » Thu Sep 07, 2006 9:11 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Nah. I would. I'd rather die than lose the ability to think for myself.

[]: Post by Timstuff » Thu Sep 07, 2006 10:12 pm I think I'll set off a shotgun in my mouth. That way there will be no more suffering. That's basically what I think of Instrumentality. It's a coward's escape from life's difficulties.

[]: Post by master0rolando » Fri Sep 08, 2006 12:35 am i would imagine myself back... of course in a world of my creation thered be some exceptionally nice..."scenery"...to keep me company :twisted:

[]: Post by Timstuff » Fri Sep 08, 2006 1:52 am Let me guess: there'd be a nice set of "twin peaks" right in front of your house, right? :P

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 08, 2006 2:09 am I'll take complementation with the Fruit of Life, but not with every single goddam human soul on the planet. Homogenous metabiological blob = teh lam0rz. If I want to go to a similar place, I'll become a monk and live in the mountains until I achieve nirvana, or whatever. Best way to ensure continued existence of human life? Pack up Yui with some syringes and send her on her way.

[]: Re: Would you have reestablished the AT-fields? Post by Faußtin » Fri Sep 08, 2006 10:04 am consrv4us wrote: One, there would be no more suffering. Two, everybody would be equal once and for all. Three, lastly without the dependency on the protien based bodies of our physical self the survival of humanity itself would almost be ensured.

[]: Re: Would you have reestablished the AT-fields? Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 08, 2006 11:48 am consrv4us wrote: One, there would be no more suffering.

[]: Re: Would you have reestablished the AT-fields? Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Sep 08, 2006 11:59 am Hunter21 wrote: consrv4us wrote: One, there would be no more suffering.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Sep 08, 2006 2:31 pm Instrumentality as shown was much more like Sartre's Inferno. The discussion seems to have drifted to something much closer to the Buddhist idea of Nirvana (or the CoE latest take on Hell). I think perspectives change with age...this snatch from an (unfinished) sf story of mine is apposite. The character is about the age I was when I wrote the piece, earlier this decade, and reflected some of my own leanings: I wrote: While she set to work on the plateful, tending to the remaining demand of the meat on her conscious activity, she felt that she would be happy to be liberated from the tyranny of guts and bladder and appetites, in some angelic state of upload - cleaner, better, upgraded. She must, she reflected, be getting old. Thinking back, she remembered when - and it must have been as a teenager, she had shared the distaste of the characters in Niven's Protector at the concept of trading the physical capacity for sex to receive enhanced intelligence and much extended lifespan. Now, she would be happy to transcend much more of her biological frame for the same outcome.

[]: Post by Rawinder » Fri Sep 08, 2006 7:00 pm Reichu wrote: I'll take complementation with the Fruit of Life

[]: Post by Six Winged Angel » Fri Sep 08, 2006 11:22 pm @would you return? no tnx

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 09, 2006 1:07 am Rawinder wrote: How exactly do you achieve complementation (i.e. "the interfusion of souls") with an organ? =P

[]: Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Sep 09, 2006 2:27 am Reichu wrote: Rawinder wrote: How exactly do you achieve complementation (i.e. "the interfusion of souls") with an organ? =P

[]: Post by Rawinder » Sat Sep 09, 2006 9:41 pm Reichu wrote: Rawinder wrote: How exactly do you achieve complementation (i.e. "the interfusion of souls") with an organ? =P

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 10, 2006 11:15 am I'm using the word a different way intentionally to trick you. But seriously, that's what hokan literally means: "complement". FoL and FoK are complementary. So, from a certain point of view, acquiring both is another form of complementation. Is it Complementation Â®? Well... I dunno. I don't see why there can't be more than one possibility. Legend of the Past wrote: So Rachel would basically like to become a SoL. Intresting choice...
